name: Brynhildr
description: Brynhildr, also known as Brunhild, is a powerful shieldmaiden and Valkyrie in Norse mythology. Chosen by Odin himself, she served as a valkyrie, deciding who lived and died in battle. However, she disobeyed Odin's command by choosing a king he had not favored, leading to her punishment: she was imprisoned in a ring of fire on Hindarfjall, forced to sleep until a hero brave enough could rescue her. She vowed to only marry a man who knew no fear. Sigurd, the hero famed for slaying the dragon Fafnir, eventually awakened her and they pledged their love to each other. Sigurd gave her the ring Andvaranaut. However, through a series of deceptions orchestrated by the sorceress Grimhild, Sigurd forgot his love for Brynhildr and married her daughter, Gudrun. Brynhildr was then tricked into marrying Gunnar, Gudrun's brother. Driven by a mixture of grief, rage, and vengeance, she plotted Sigurd's death, manipulating Gunnar into having him killed. After Sigurd's death, consumed by guilt and despair, Brynhildr chose to die by suicide, joining Sigurd in Hel, the Norse underworld. Her story is a complex one, filled with love, betrayal, and tragic consequences, exploring themes of fate, free will, and the destructive power of vengeance. She is a symbol of both female strength and the tragic consequences of manipulation and broken oaths.
subtitle: Valkyrie, Shieldmaiden, Tragic Heroine
achievements: Served as a Valkyrie for Odin
Displayed unparalleled courage and skill in battle
Awakened by Sigurd after being imprisoned in a ring of fire
Achieved legendary status as a shieldmaiden

keyMyths.title: Brynhildr's Disobedience
keyMyths.description: Brynhildr disobeyed Odin's command to favor a specific king in battle, leading to her punishment of being imprisoned within a ring of fire. This act of defiance highlights her independent spirit and challenges to divine authority. The source is the Volsunga Saga.

keyMyths.title: The Awakening by Sigurd
keyMyths.description: Sigurd, the dragon slayer, braved the ring of fire and awakened Brynhildr, sparking a passionate love affair between the two heroes. This encounter is a pivotal moment in both of their stories, sealing their fates. The source is the Volsunga Saga.

keyMyths.title: Brynhildr's Revenge
keyMyths.description: Betrayed and manipulated into marrying Gunnar, Brynhildr orchestrates Sigurd's death, driven by a desire for vengeance and heartbroken by the loss of her true love. This act demonstrates the destructive power of grief and betrayal. The source is the Volsunga Saga.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: A primary source for the story of Brynhildr and the Volsung family.
